metadata.description: Full deep-research shopping workflow. Takes a user shopping query through intent parsing, optional clarification, multi-source search (16 e-commerce sites + 4 community forums via shopping_search), review collection, deal analysis, recommendation synthesis, and Telegram-optimized delivery. shopping_search handles scraping, matching, and community data automatically — LLM steps focus on analysis, judgment, and presentation.

phases.name: Understand Query
phases.goal: Parse intent, extract constraints, check if clarification is needed.

phases.name: Clarify If Needed
phases.goal: Ask clarifying questions if query is too vague, otherwise pass through.

phases.name: Search & Collect Reviews
phases.goal: Run shopping_search, then fetch detailed reviews for top products.

phases.name: Analyze Value & Risks
phases.goal: Score deals, flag review red flags, give buy/wait timing advice.

phases.name: Synthesize Recommendation
phases.goal: Build tiered recommendation: Best Overall, Budget Pick, alternatives.

phases.name: Format & Deliver
phases.goal: Format recommendation as a polished Telegram-ready message.

steps.name: understand_query_check_clarity
steps.title: Understand Query & Check Clarity
steps.instruction: Read the user's query. Determine: is this a specific product lookup (e.g. 'siemens s100') or a vague exploration (e.g. 'good coffee machine')? Extract product name, category, budget if mentioned, brand preferences. Check user profile for past preferences. If the query is too vague to search effectively (no product category, no brand, no use case), set needs_clarification=true and list 1-3 specific questions. If the query is clear enough, set needs_clarification=false.
steps.done_when: parsed_intent has product/category/constraints extracted, or needs_clarification is set.

steps.name: ask_clarifying_questions
steps.title: Ask Clarifying Questions
steps.instruction:
Read parsed_intent and user_query. Decide: is this query specific enough to research directly, or would 2-3 questions help narrow the search?

SPECIFIC (research directly): brand+model ('siemens s100'), exact product ('dyson v15'), specific comparison ('iphone 15 vs samsung s24'). Return the query as clarified_query immediately.

NEEDS CLARIFICATION: generic category ('coffee machines', '3d printers', 'laptop'), broad brand ('samsung phones'). Ask 2-3 questions about budget, use case, and key constraint. Use the needs_clarification response to pause and ask the user via Telegram.

IMPORTANT: If the query is specific, do NOT ask questions. Just return it.
steps.done_when: clarified_query is ready for search — either the original specific query or the user's answers incorporated.

steps.name: search_and_collect_reviews
steps.title: Search & Collect Reviews
steps.instruction: Call shopping_search with the query from clarified_query (or user_query if clarification was skipped). The tool returns products from 16 e-commerce sites + community data from 4 forums. Then for the top 2-3 product URLs in the results, call shopping_fetch_reviews to get detailed reviews. If shopping_search returned few/no products, use web_search as a supplement for manufacturer specs or international retailers. Combine everything into search_results (products + community) and review_data (detailed reviews from URLs). Do NOT fabricate any data.
steps.done_when: search_results has products with real prices from the tool, review_data has reviews from at least 1 source.

steps.name: analyze_value_and_risks
steps.title: Analyze Value & Risks
steps.instruction: Read search_results and review_data. Evaluate: Are any discounts fake (original price inflated)? Are there red flags in reviews (recurring defects, complaints on Şikayetvar)? Is now a good time to buy or should the user wait for a sale? Use shopping_compare for value scoring if multiple products. Use shopping_reviews to synthesize review themes if raw reviews available. Produce a deal_analysis with: value verdict per product, risk flags, timing recommendation.
steps.done_when: deal_analysis covers value scoring, risk flags, and buy/wait timing.

steps.name: synthesize_recommendation
steps.title: Synthesize Recommendation
steps.instruction: Read all artifacts. Build a tiered recommendation: Best Overall (best value score + good reviews), Budget Pick (lowest price meeting requirements), and any alternatives. Use shopping_alternatives if the user might benefit from different brands or previous-gen models. Use shopping_timing for category-specific buy timing advice. Include warnings from deal_analysis. Structure as clear readable text with product names, prices, sources, pros/cons.
steps.done_when: recommendation has top pick, budget option, and timing advice.

steps.name: format_and_deliver
steps.title: Format & Deliver
steps.instruction: Take the recommendation artifact and format it for Telegram delivery. Lead with a one-line answer to the user's original query. Present top pick with price and where to buy. Then budget option if different. Then alternatives. Then warnings. Then timing advice. Keep it scannable — use bold for product names and prices, bullet points for comparisons. End with 'Watch price' / 'Compare more' / 'Ask me' action hints.
steps.done_when: shopping_response is a polished, user-facing Telegram message.
